changes_summary: Retargeted the plan to cover every live single-task cache entry point, and split `useTaskStatusCounts` into current-project store narrowing plus query-authoritative all-project mode so freshness stays correct without widening realtime subscriptions.

success_criteria.criterion: A new `src/shared/state/realtimeStore.ts` exists with effective-project task storage, full-record upsert/reset actions, and selector helpers for single-task, pending-generation, and current-project aggregate task views.
Cold-start hydration is explicitly implemented through centralized query or repository paths so current-project task details and pending-task surfaces are correct before the first new realtime event.

`RealtimeProvider` writes incoming task realtime updates into the store at the boundary while preserving the existing selected-project Supabase connection lifecycle and the status-only `useRealtime` API.

`useGetTask` preserves the empty-id disabled contract and `resolveTaskProjectScope()` fallback behavior while reading from the store when available and staying React-Query-authoritative for fetch lifecycle.

Every live single-task cache writer or reader that uses `taskQueryKeys.single(...)` is either integrated into store seeding or explicitly deferred with rationale and test impact, including `useTaskPrefetch`, `generationTaskCache`, and `useVariantSourceTask`.

`usePendingGenerationTasks` and `useGenerationTaskDetails` read from narrow store selectors or the adapted single-task path instead of broad polling or unrelated invalidation for visible current-project task-state updates.
`useTaskStatusCounts` preserves current derivation semantics, including root-task exclusion, visible task-type filtering, processing-status rules, and recent success or failure windows.
`useTaskStatusCounts({ allProjectIds })` remains correct by keeping a query-authoritative freshness path rather than relying on selected-project realtime store updates for non-selected projects.
No task-state consumer imports `RealtimeContext` or a broadcast-style realtime task API after migration; `useRealtime` may remain only as an intentional status-only API.
Selectors that return objects or arrays provide stable references for semantically unchanged results, using `shallow` or equivalent equality control, and tests cover that behavior.

Store and hook tests cover task upsert, scoped reset, selector narrowing for unrelated task updates, aggregate status-count derivation, `resolveTaskProjectScope()` fallback behavior, empty-id disabled behavior, and coherent store seeding from prefetch or warmup helpers.
Existing public hook signatures remain stable enough that downstream callers in gallery items, variant details, Tasks Pane, lightbox, and task details require little or no API churn.
`useActiveTaskClips` is explicitly classified as deferred or migrated with rationale so the milestone scope remains intentional.
Gallery items no longer rerender on unrelated task events in a 50+ item runtime smoke test.
A per-task status surface, if present after migration, rerenders only for the matching task when observed with runtime telemetry.

assumptions: `src/shared/state/createEntityStore.ts` still exists as a local reference pattern, but `realtimeStore.ts` will be purpose-built around effective-project task snapshots rather than a generic entity abstraction.
`RealtimeProvider` remains the selected-project realtime boundary and `RealtimeConnection` will continue subscribing to one `task-updates:${projectId}` channel at a time.
React Query remains authoritative for server truth and fetch lifecycle; the store exists only to narrow subscriptions and hold normalized task snapshots for visible UI consumers.
All-project task counts must remain query-authoritative unless subscription coverage changes in a later milestone, because the current realtime boundary cannot keep non-selected projects fresh.

Direct single-task cache helpers such as prefetch, generation-task cache warmup, and variant-source task loading may continue to use React Query so long as successful fetches seed the store through shared centralized helpers.
`useActiveTaskClips` remains outside M3 unless a final implementation can adopt an already-built selector helper with no added semantics, no widened scope, and negligible extra tests.
